title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: facpart installable R package providing tools partitioning 2-dimensional point configurations (multidimensional-scaling output) according Facet Theory. implements three partition types — axial (parallel lines), radial (nested circles ellipses), angular (wedge sectors) — plus ellipse-fitting utilities. git repository rooted facpart/. standalone package; dependency script files outside directory.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: development commands run inside facpart/ directory (facpart.Rproj open RStudio): edit NAMESPACE hand — auto-generated roxygen2. Run test suite : Currently guttman.R functions formal tests (tests/testthat/test-guttman.R). Ad-hoc testing functions done interactively via devtools::load_all() followed manual calls.
code: devtools::load_all()   # load all functions without installing (fastest for interactive dev) devtools::document()   # regenerate man/ and NAMESPACE from roxygen2 comments devtools::install()    # install locally devtools::check()      # run R CMD check devtools::build()      # build the source tarball (facpart_x.y.z.tar.gz) devtools::test()                                          # all tests testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-guttman.R")     # single file

title: CLAUDE.md
text: man/ generated devtools::document() edited directly.
code: facpart/ ├── DESCRIPTION               package metadata ├── NAMESPACE                 auto-generated; do not edit ├── LICENSE / LICENSE.md      MIT ├── README.md ├── .Rbuildignore ├── data/                     bundled datasets (gutt91.rda, guttman65mds.rda) └── R/     ├── facpart-package.R     package-level docs + all @importFrom directives (no code)     ├── data.R                roxygen2 docs for bundled datasets     ├── ellipse.R             ellipseInConfig(), inoutEllipse()     ├── axial.R               axialLine(), axialLines()     ├── radial-circle.R       radialCircle(), radialCircles() + internal helpers     ├── radial-ellipse.R      radialEllipse(), radialEllipses() + internal helpers     ├── angular.R             angularPartition()     ├── guttman.R             mu2(), mu2df()     └── utils.R               .permutations(), .assign_groups() + highlightMisclass()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: DESCRIPTION: Depends: R (>= 4.0), plotrix — draw.circle(), draw.ellipse() Imports: cluster (ellipsoidhull()), MASS (lda()), grDevices, graphics, stats, utils Suggests: smacof (datasets used examples), knitr, rmarkdown (vignette), testthat >= 3.0.0 (tests)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: partition ellipse-drawing functions draw currently active plot window. Call plot() functions. inoutEllipse(), mu2(), mu2df() output parameter — always return value directly. highlightMisclass() output parameter either — pure side-effect always returns invisible(NULL). exported functions follow output = TRUE/FALSE convention.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: radial-ellipse.R calls .best_radius() defined radial-circle.R. cross-file dependency exists two places: independent-center ellipse path (nesting constraint) fixed-shape radialEllipses() path (scale-factor scan). loading package invisible, sourcing files interactively, source radial-circle.R first. Private helpers use . prefix (e.g., .circle_pts, .in_ellipse, .arc_mid) tagged #' @noRd. internal file.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: "maxdist" (default): covariance-based ellipse centered data centroid, scaled maximum Mahalanobis distance + 5% buffer. "bestfit": minimises sum squared normalised elliptic distances via BFGS; initial guess covariance ellipse. "minbound": exact minimax minimum-area enclosing ellipse via ellipsoidhull(). ellipseInConfig() supports mid = "centroid" (default) mid = "nullnull" (forces center origin). angle ellipse output radians.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: axialLine(): binary split. Classical LDA Bayes-rule boundary — perpendicular LD1 midpoint class means. Requires exactly 2 group levels. Vertical-line guard: |w[2]| < 1e-10, slope = Inf intercept carries x-position. Returns predicted (LDA class factor); function . axialLines(): k ≥ 2 groups, k−1 parallel lines. Joint search angle cuts: grid n_angles angles [0, π) plus LDA’s LD1 direction; angle, exhaustive enumeration C(n−1, k−1) cut placements. Ties broken largest minimum margin (perpendicular distance cut line nearest point). vertical-line guard axialLine(). cut search vectorised via cumulative per-group counts (cum[+1, g] = count group g among first sorted points): segment’s group counts vector subtraction cum[hi+1,] - cum[lo+1,], C(n−1, k−1) candidate partitions scored together pmax/pmin — per-combo R loop, per-segment tabulate(). ~17–23× faster previous per-combo loop k ≥ 3 returning bit-identical results (enumeration tie-break, .max keeps first--scan-order winner). “simplify” back nested loop combn() columns. use : use axialLine() classical LDA classifier (optimal Gaussian equal-covariance classes); use axialLines() empirically best parallel-line separator (can find non-LD1 direction beats LDA training sample).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: radialCircle() / radialCircles(): nested (inclusive) circles. Center optimised multi-start Nelder-Mead parscale set data range. Starts include overall centroid, group’s centroid, (radialCircles()) previous circle’s center. Nesting constraint r_s ≥ dist(center_s, center_{s−1}) + r_{s−1} enforced via minimum radius brute-force radius scan (.best_radius()). misclass plain integer. Supplying cx, cy radialCircles() makes circles concentric.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: radialEllipse() / radialEllipses(): elliptic analogue radial circle functions. ellipse 5 parameters (cx, cy, , b, angle) optimised Nelder-Mead parscale = c(x_range, y_range, max_range, max_range, pi). Multi-start includes covariance ellipse inner groups plus inflated copy previous ellipse. misclass list (n, indices) — count plus row indices. Angles radians internally; converted degrees draw.ellipse(). ellipse (5-vector) supplied radialEllipses(), outer ellipses uniform scalings sharing fixed center/orientation/ratio; scale factors found exact 1D scan via .best_radius(). misclass type differs circle functions: radialEllipse() / radialEllipses() return misclass list list(n, indices); radialCircle() / radialCircles() return misclass plain integer.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mu2(): Guttman’s mu2 two vectors. Computed sum(dx * dy) / sum(|dx| * |dy|) pairs (, j), dx = x_i - x_j. Returns values [−1, 1]; NaN differences zero. Uses pairwise NA deletion. mu2df(): Applies mu2() column pairs data frame. Returns full symmetric matrix (as_dist = FALSE, default) dist object sqrt(1 − mu2) values (as_dist = TRUE). Unlike partition functions, plot side-effect.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: angularPartition(): k wedge sectors common center. Brute-force n × C(n−1, k−1) candidate angle k-tuples; tie-broken largest minimum 2D distance cut ray nearest point. Center optimised either cx cy NULL (default) via multi-start Nelder-Mead; starts overall centroid plus group’s centroid. Ray length computed par("usr"). angularPartition3() (fixed k=3 reference) exists companion script layer part package. exported drawing functions accept add = TRUE (default, draw existing plot) add = FALSE (call plot() first). ellipseInConfig() labels points add = FALSE; functions also draw group labels via graphics::text().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Use _ . object names (e.g., grp_int, grp.int). Indentation: 4 spaces. use ggplot2 plots; graphics use base R. Never use function names object names (e.g., avoid levels <- levels(x)). Private helpers prefixed . tagged #' @noRd. add top-level require() calls — package imports handle dependencies. Every exported function returns invisible(NULL) output = FALSE; package-wide convention. ellipseInConfig() labs parameter currently unused kept backward compatibility — remove .

code: @Manual{,   title = {facpart: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations},   author = {Hans-Ruediger Pfister},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.1},   url = {https://rpfister57.github.io/facpart/}, }

title: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations
text: facpart package functions build Facet Theory. helps partition 2-dimensional point configurations (multidimensional-scaling output) according typical facet theoretical partition patterns. three types partion schemes: Axial — parallel separating lines: axialLine(), axialLines() Radial — nested circles ellipses: radialCircle(), radialCircles(), radialEllipse(), radialEllipses() Angular — wedge-shaped sectors: angularPartition() Plus utilities: ellipseInConfig(), inoutEllipse(), mu2

title: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations
text: Guttman, R., & Greenbaum, C. W. (1998). Facet theory: development current status. European Psychologist, 3(1), 13-36. Shye, S. (2014). Faceted Smallest Space Analysis (FSSA). . Michalos (Ed.), Encyclopedia quality life research (pp. 2129-2133). New York: Springer. Shye, S. (2015). New directions facet theory. S. Shye, E. Solomon, & . Borg (Eds.), 15th International Facet Theory Conference (pp. 147-158). New York City: Fordham University.

title: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations
text: current version can installed github. Note: facpart early stage!
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github(repo = "https://github.com/rpfister57/facpart.git")

title: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations
code: library(facpart)  set.seed(1) # Three clusters arranged around a common center theta <- rep(c(0, 2 * pi / 3, 4 * pi / 3), each = 12) + rnorm(36, 0, 0.25) r     <- runif(36, 0.5, 1.5) crd   <- cbind(r * cos(theta), r * sin(theta)) grp   <- factor(rep(c("a", "b", "c"), each = 12))  plot(crd, asp = 1) res <- angularPartition(crd, grp) res$misclass   # number of misclassified points res$center     # optimised wedge apex

title: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations
text: cluster — minimum-area enclosing ellipse via ellipsoidhull() MASS — lda() axial partitions plotrix — draw.circle(), draw.ellipse() functionality uses base R. package works well smacof package.

title: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations
text: MIT

what: Angular k-way (wedge) partition of a 2D configuration — angularPartition
title: Angular k-way (wedge) partition of a 2D configuration — angularPartition
text: Finds k rays (straight lines) emanating center point partition 2D configuration n points k angular sectors, minimising total misclassification (points whose group differs majority group sector), draws lines. (see: Shye, S. (2014). Faceted Smallest Space Analysis (FSSA). . Michalos (Ed.), Encyclopedia quality life research (pp. 2129-2133). New York: Springer.)

title: Angular k-way (wedge) partition of a 2D configuration — angularPartition
code: angularPartition(   crd,   group,   cx = NULL,   cy = NULL,   output = TRUE,   col = "darkorange",   lwd = 2,   lty = 1,   add = TRUE )

title: Angular k-way (wedge) partition of a 2D configuration — angularPartition
text: crd Numeric matrix numeric data frame exactly 2 columns; NAs. group Factor n >= k >= 2 levels, length nrow crd. cx, cy Center; optimised either NULL. output TRUE (default), return list results. col Ray colour (default "darkorange"). lwd Line width (default 2). lty Line type (default 1). add TRUE (default), add existing plot, else plot configuration.

title: Angular k-way (wedge) partition of a 2D configuration — angularPartition
text: output = TRUE, list : cuts — numeric[k], cut angles rays (radians). margin — minimum distance cut ray nearest point. misclass — integer, number misclassified points. misclass_points — data frame (x, y, label) misclassified points sector — integer[n], assigned sector 1..k per point majority — character[k], majority group per sector center — c(cx, cy) center coordinates rays pt_angles — angles (radians) points center

title: Angular k-way (wedge) partition of a 2D configuration — angularPartition
text: Search fixed center. Points sorted angle center. partition choice k cut-gaps among n gaps consecutive points (combn(n, k) candidates); per-arc majority counts read cumulative count table total misclassification minimised. Cut angles placed arc midpoint adjacent points. Tie-breaker: among k-tuples misclass count, one largest minimum 2D perpendicular distance cut ray nearest point wins. Search optimal center. cx cy NULL (default), center optimised multi-start Nelder-Mead — brute-force runs inner objective candidate center. Starts data centroid plus centroid non-empty group. parscale set data range. cx cy supplied, used directly (optimisation).

title: Angular k-way (wedge) partition of a 2D configuration — angularPartition
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(123) theta <- rep(c(0, 2 * pi / 3, 4 * pi / 3), each = 12) + rnorm(36, 0, 0.25) r     <- runif(36, 0.5, 1.5) crd   <- cbind(r * cos(theta), r * sin(theta)) grp   <- factor(rep(c("a", "b", "c"), each = 12)) plot(crd, asp = 1) angularPartition(crd, grp) } # }

what: Binary LDA separating line for a 2D point configuration — axialLine
title: Binary LDA separating line for a 2D point configuration — axialLine
text: Fits linear discriminant analysis two-group 2D data draws classical LDA boundary — perpendicular LD1 midpoint class means — current plot.

title: Binary LDA separating line for a 2D point configuration — axialLine
code: axialLine(   crd,   group,   fill = FALSE,   output = TRUE,   col = "purple",   cols = c("steelblue", "tomato"),   lwd = 2,   lty = 1,   add = TRUE )

title: Binary LDA separating line for a 2D point configuration — axialLine
text: crd Numeric matrix data frame exactly 2 columns (x, y). group Factor, character, integer vector exactly 2 levels. fill TRUE, shade two half-planes (default FALSE). output TRUE (default), return list line parameters. col Separator line colour (default "purple"). cols Length-2 fill colours; used fill = TRUE (default c("steelblue", "tomato")). lwd Line width (default 2). lty Line type (default 1). add TRUE (default), add existing plot; FALSE, call plot() first.

title: Binary LDA separating line for a 2D point configuration — axialLine
text: output = TRUE, list slope, intercept, misclass (integer), misclass_points (data frame columns x, y, label misclassified point), predicted (LDA-predicted class factor).

title: Binary LDA separating line for a 2D point configuration — axialLine
text: LD1 along x-axis (abs(w[2]) < 1e-10) separator vertical: slope returned Inf intercept carries x-position line. special case axialLines(). axialLine() returns classical LDA Bayes-rule boundary (closed-form: LD1 direction, midpoint class means cut) — optimal multivariate-normal classes equal covariances, standard reference object psychometric / Facet Theory pipelines. return value includes predicted, LDA class assignment per point. axialLines() k = 2, contrast, searches full (angle, cut) space empirical minimum-misclass parallel line. direction need LD1 cut need match midpoint means; can overfit training sample classes non-Gaussian unequal covariances. Use want empirically best linear separator; use axialLine() want classical LDA classifier.

title: Binary LDA separating line for a 2D point configuration — axialLine
code: set.seed(1) crd <- rbind(cbind(rnorm(15, -1), rnorm(15)),              cbind(rnorm(15,  1), rnorm(15))) grp <- factor(c(rep("a", 15), rep("b", 15))) axialLine(crd, grp, fill = TRUE, add = FALSE)  #> $slope #> [1] -7.867212 #>  #> $intercept #>         2  #> 0.8714933  #>  #> $misclass #> [1] 4 #>  #> $misclass_points #>            x           y label #> 1  0.5952808  0.82122120     a #> 2  0.5117812 -0.05612874     a #> 3  0.1249309  0.41794156     a #> 4 -0.3770596  0.88110773     b #>  #> $predicted #>  [1] a a a b a a a a a a b a a a b b b b b a b b b b b b b b b b #> Levels: a b #>

what: Multi-group parallel-line partition (angle + cuts searched) — axialLines
title: Multi-group parallel-line partition (angle + cuts searched) — axialLines
text: Partitions 2D configuration k >= 2 groups using k-1 parallel separating lines. slopes lines positions searched minimise empirical misclassification:

title: Multi-group parallel-line partition (angle + cuts searched) — axialLines
code: axialLines(   crd,   group,   fill = FALSE,   output = TRUE,   col = "purple",   cols = NULL,   lwd = 2,   lty = 1,   n_angles = 180L,   add = TRUE )

title: Multi-group parallel-line partition (angle + cuts searched) — axialLines
text: crd Numeric matrix data frame exactly 2 columns. group Factor, character, integer vector k >= 2 levels. fill TRUE, shade k strips consecutive lines. output TRUE (default), return list results. col Separator line colour (default "purple"). cols Length-k fill colours; auto-generated NULL. lwd Line width (default 2). lty Line type (default 1). n_angles Number angles [0, pi) scan (default 180L, approximately 1-degree resolution); LDA's direction added extra candidate. add TRUE (default), add existing plot; FALSE, call plot() first.

title: Multi-group parallel-line partition (angle + cuts searched) — axialLines
text: output = TRUE, list : slope — shared slope lines (Inf vertical) intercepts — numeric[k-1], y-intercepts (x-positions vertical) angle — winning direction radians margin — minimum perpendicular distance cut line(s) nearest point misclass — total misclassified points misclass_points — data frame (x, y, label) misclassified points sector — integer[n], sector 1..k per point input order majority — character[k], majority group per sector

title: Multi-group parallel-line partition (angle + cuts searched) — axialLines
text: Angle: grid n_angles equally-spaced angles [0, pi), augmented LDA's LD1 direction. angle theta, points projected onto (cos theta, sin theta). Cuts: angle, exact brute-force enumeration C(n-1, k-1) ways split sorted projections k segments. (angle, cuts) combination lowest total misclassification wins. Tie-breaker: among configurations misclass count, one largest minimum margin (perpendicular distance cut line nearest point) preferred — keeps cut lines visually away data points. k = 2, axialLines() can differ axialLine(): latter places cut midpoint class means LD1 projection (classical Bayes rule normality), axialLines() searches full (angle, cut) space empirical optimum. Vertical-line guard: winning direction w[2] ~= 0, separators vertical. slope returned Inf intercepts carry x-positions lines.

title: Multi-group parallel-line partition (angle + cuts searched) — axialLines

what: Fit and draw an ellipse around a 2D configuration — ellipseInConfig
title: Fit and draw an ellipse around a 2D configuration — ellipseInConfig
text: Three fitting strategies available:

title: Fit and draw an ellipse around a 2D configuration — ellipseInConfig
code: ellipseInConfig(   crd,   labs = NULL,   mid = "centroid",   typ = "maxdist",   output = TRUE,   col = "black",   fill = NA,   lwd = 2,   lty = 2,   add = TRUE )

title: Fit and draw an ellipse around a 2D configuration — ellipseInConfig
text: crd Numeric matrix data frame exactly 2 columns (x, y). labs Currently unused (kept backward compatibility). mid "centroid" (default) places ellipse center data centroid; "nullnull" forces center origin (0, 0). affects "maxdist". typ Fitting strategy: "maxdist" (default), "bestfit", "minbound". output TRUE (default), return list describing ellipse. col Border colour (default "black"). fill Fill colour, NA (default) fill. lwd Line width (default 2). lty Line type (default 2). add TRUE (default), add existing plot; FALSE, call plot() first.

title: Fit and draw an ellipse around a 2D configuration — ellipseInConfig
text: output = TRUE, list elements cx, cy, , b, angle (radians).

title: Fit and draw an ellipse around a 2D configuration — ellipseInConfig
text: "maxdist" — covariance-based ellipse, scaled points lie inside 5% buffer. Semi-axes sqrt(eigenvalue) * k, k maximum Mahalanobis distance center. "bestfit" — minimises sum squared normalised elliptic distances sqrt((u/)^2 + (v/b)^2) - 1 via BFGS, starting covariance ellipse. "minbound" — minimum-area enclosing ellipse via cluster::ellipsoidhull(). ellipse drawn currently active plot window.

title: Fit and draw an ellipse around a 2D configuration — ellipseInConfig
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) crd <- cbind(rnorm(30), 0.5 * rnorm(30)) ellipseInConfig(crd, typ = "maxdist", add = FALSE) ellipseInConfig(crd, typ = "bestfit", col = "blue") ellipseInConfig(crd, typ = "minbound", col = "red") } # }

what: facpart: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations — facpart-package
title: facpart: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations — facpart-package
text: Tools partitioning 2-dimensional configurations (multidimensional-scaling output) facets, supporting axial (parallel lines), radial (nested circles ellipses), angular (wedge-shaped) partitions. Includes utilities fitting bounding, least-squares, minimum-area ellipses.

title: facpart: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations — facpart-package
text: axialLine(), axialLines() — parallel-line partitions radialCircle(), radialCircles() — nested-circle partitions radialEllipse(), radialEllipses() — nested-ellipse partitions angularPartition() — wedge partitions

title: facpart: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations — facpart-package
text: ellipseInConfig() — fit draw bounding/best-fit/minimum ellipse inoutEllipse() — classify points inside/outside given ellipse

title: facpart: Facet Theory Partitioning of 2D Configurations — facpart-package
text: highlightMisclass() — overlay markers misclassified points

title: Guttman 1991 Intellligence Data — gutt91
text: Data based smacof package. contains correlations among 12 intelligence items, assignment two facets: Material Modality.

title: Guttman 1991 Intellligence Data — gutt91
code: gutt91

title: Guttman 1991 Intellligence Data — gutt91
text: list two elements: gutt91_cor, gutt91_var gutt91_cor correlation matrix 12 intelligence items gutt91_var data frame 12 observations 3 variables: items = short name item, Material = Facet 1 (Oral, Manual, Paper), Modality = Facet 2 (verbal, numerical, figural)

title: Guttman 1991 Intellligence Data — gutt91
text: smacof package, data(Guttman1991)

title: Guttman 1991 Intellligence Data — gutt91
text: Guttman, L. & Levy, S. (1991). Two structural laws intelligence tests. Intelligence, 15, 79-103.

title: Guttman 1991 Intellligence Data — gutt91
code: data(gutt91) Kor <- gutt91$gutt91_cor Facets <- gutt91$gutt91_var # convert correlations to distances Kor_D <- smacof::sim2diss(Kor, method = "corr", to.dist = TRUE) # MDS gutt91_mds <- smacof::mds(Kor_D, type = "ordinal") plot(gutt91_mds, main = "Guttman 1991 Intelligence") # Angular partition of Modality angularPartition(crd = gutt91_mds$conf, group = Facets$Modality)  #> $cuts #> Comprehension         Mazes    Arithmetic  #>     -1.645176      1.049505     -3.076990  #>  #> $margin #> [1] 0.1103395 #>  #> $misclass #> [1] 0 #>  #> $misclass_points #> [1] x     y     label #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names) #>  #> $sector #>  [1] 2 2 1 2 2 1 3 3 3 3 1 3 #>  #> $majority #> [1] "numerical" "verbal"    "figural"   #>  #> $center #> [1]  1.838807e-16 -1.966698e-17 #>  #> $pt_angles #>        Information       Similarities         Arithmetic         Vocabulary  #>         -2.6020094         -2.0511100          2.7780239         -2.6488194  #>      Comprehension          DigitSpan  PictureCompletion PictureArrangement  #>         -2.0000848          2.6312076         -1.2902663         -0.9209234  #>        BlockDesign     ObjectAssembly             Coding              Mazes  #>          0.2315753         -0.3157876          1.7483111          0.3506980  #>

title: Highlight misclassified points on the active plot — highlightMisclass
text: invisible(NULL), called side effect drawing active plot.

what: Guttman's weak monotonicity coefficient mu2 — mu2
title: Guttman's weak monotonicity coefficient mu2 — mu2
text: Computes Guttman's mu2 coefficient measuring degree weak monotone relationship two numeric vectors. Defined HUDAP Manual (2001, p. 219). Pairs either value NA excluded (pairwise deletion).

title: Guttman's weak monotonicity coefficient mu2 — mu2
code: mu2(x, y)

title: Guttman's weak monotonicity coefficient mu2 — mu2
text: x Numeric vector. y Numeric vector length x.

title: Guttman's weak monotonicity coefficient mu2 — mu2
text: single numeric value [-1, 1]. Returns NaN pairs tied (zero absolute differences).

title: Guttman's weak monotonicity coefficient mu2 — mu2

what: Matrix of mu2 coefficients for a data frame — mu2df
title: Matrix of mu2 coefficients for a data frame — mu2df
text: Computes pairwise Guttman mu2 coefficients numeric columns data frame, returning either full symmetric correlation matrix lower-triangular distance matrix (1 - mu2).

title: Matrix of mu2 coefficients for a data frame — mu2df
code: mu2df(df, as_dist = FALSE)

title: Matrix of mu2 coefficients for a data frame — mu2df
text: df data frame (coercible object) numeric columns. as_dist TRUE, return lower-triangular dist object 1 - mu2 values. FALSE (default), return full symmetric correlation matrix.

title: Matrix of mu2 coefficients for a data frame — mu2df
text: square numeric matrix dimnames set column names df (as_dist = FALSE), dist object Labels set column names (as_dist = TRUE).

title: Matrix of mu2 coefficients for a data frame — mu2df

what: Binary radial partition (one separating circle) — radialCircle
title: Binary radial partition (one separating circle) — radialCircle
text: Finds circle minimising misclassification two groups 2D points. center found multi-start Nelder-Mead (starts: data centroid plus group's centroid) unless cx cy supplied. given center, optimal radius found linear scan n-1 candidate midpoints without assuming group inner.

title: Binary radial partition (one separating circle) — radialCircle
code: radialCircle(   crd,   group,   cx = NULL,   cy = NULL,   fill = FALSE,   output = TRUE,   col = "purple",   cols = c("steelblue", "tomato"),   lwd = 2,   lty = 1,   .method = "Nelder-Mead",   add = TRUE )

title: Binary radial partition (one separating circle) — radialCircle
text: crd Numeric matrix data frame exactly 2 columns. group Factor, character, integer vector exactly 2 levels. cx, cy Center separating circle; optimised NULL. fill TRUE, shade inner disc outer region. output TRUE (default), return results list. col Circle border colour (default "purple"). cols Length-2 fill colours (default c("steelblue", "tomato")). lwd Line width (default 2). lty Line type (default 1). .method "Nelder-Mead" (default) "SANN". add TRUE (default), add existing plot; FALSE, call plot() first.

title: Binary radial partition (one separating circle) — radialCircle
text: output = TRUE, list center, radius, misclass (integer), misclass_points (data frame columns x, y, label misclassified point), sector (1 inside / 2 outside per point), majority (character[2]).

title: Binary radial partition (one separating circle) — radialCircle
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) inner <- cbind(rnorm(20, 0, 0.3), rnorm(20, 0, 0.3)) th <- runif(20, 0, 2 * pi) outer <- cbind(2 * cos(th), 2 * sin(th)) + matrix(rnorm(40, 0, 0.1), 20) crd <- rbind(inner, outer) grp <- factor(c(rep("in", 20), rep("out", 20))) radialCircle(crd, grp, fill = TRUE, add = FALSE) } # }

title: K-group radial partition (nested circles) — radialCircles
text: Generalises radialCircle() k >= 2 groups using k-1 nested (inclusive) circles. circles must nested (circle s contains circle s-1). Groups ordered factor level (level 1 = innermost). Circles fitted sequentially, minimising misclassification groups 1..s (inside) vs groups s+1..k (outside).

title: K-group radial partition (nested circles) — radialCircles
code: radialCircles(   crd,   group,   cx = NULL,   cy = NULL,   fill = FALSE,   output = TRUE,   col = "purple",   cols = NULL,   lwd = 2,   lty = 1,   .method = "Nelder-Mead",   add = TRUE )

title: K-group radial partition (nested circles) — radialCircles
text: crd Numeric matrix data frame exactly 2 columns. group Factor k >= 2 levels (factor levels define inside--outside ordering). cx, cy Optional shared center k-1 circles. supplied circles concentric (cx, cy); otherwise center optimised. fill TRUE, shade ring sector. output TRUE (default), return results list. col Circle border colour (default "purple"). cols Length-k colour vector; auto-generated NULL. lwd Line width (default 2). lty Line type (default 1). .method "Nelder-Mead" (default) "SANN"; ignored cx cy supplied. add TRUE (default), add existing plot; FALSE, call plot() first.

title: K-group radial partition (nested circles) — radialCircles
text: output = TRUE, list cx, cy (numeric[k-1]), radii (innermost outermost), misclass, misclass_points (data frame columns x, y, label misclassified point), sector (integer[n]), majority (character[k]).

title: K-group radial partition (nested circles) — radialCircles
text: cx cy supplied, k-1 circles share center (concentric); radii searched, subject r_s >= r_{s-1}. either NULL (default), circle's center optimised independently multi-start Nelder-Mead — circles nested generally concentric.

title: K-group radial partition (nested circles) — radialCircles
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) g1 <- cbind(rnorm(15, 0, 0.2), rnorm(15, 0, 0.2)) th2 <- runif(15, 0, 2 * pi) g2 <- cbind(1.2 * cos(th2), 1.2 * sin(th2)) + matrix(rnorm(30, 0, 0.1), 15) th3 <- runif(15, 0, 2 * pi) g3 <- cbind(2.5 * cos(th3), 2.5 * sin(th3)) + matrix(rnorm(30, 0, 0.1), 15) crd <- rbind(g1, g2, g3) grp <- factor(c(rep("a", 15), rep("b", 15), rep("c", 15))) radialCircles(crd, grp, fill = TRUE, add = FALSE) } # }

what: Binary radial-elliptic partition (one separating ellipse) — radialEllipse
title: Binary radial-elliptic partition (one separating ellipse) — radialEllipse
text: Finds ellipse minimising misclassification two groups 2D points. 5 parameters (cx, cy, , b, angle) found Nelder-Mead, starting covariance ellipse group turn; ordering lower misclassification retained.

title: Binary radial-elliptic partition (one separating ellipse) — radialEllipse
code: radialEllipse(   crd,   group,   fill = FALSE,   output = TRUE,   col = "purple",   cols = c("steelblue", "tomato"),   lwd = 2,   lty = 1,   add = TRUE )

title: Binary radial-elliptic partition (one separating ellipse) — radialEllipse
text: crd Numeric matrix data frame exactly 2 columns. group Factor exactly 2 levels. fill TRUE, shade inner ellipse outer region. output TRUE (default), return results list. col Ellipse border colour (default "purple"). cols Length-2 fill colours (default c("steelblue", "tomato")). lwd Line width (default 2). lty Line type (default 1). add TRUE (default), add existing plot; FALSE, call plot() first.

title: Binary radial-elliptic partition (one separating ellipse) — radialEllipse
text: output = TRUE, list cx, cy, , b, angle (radians), misclass (list n indices), misclass_points (data frame columns x, y, label misclassified point), sector, majority.

title: Binary radial-elliptic partition (one separating ellipse) — radialEllipse
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) inner <- cbind(rnorm(20, 0, 0.3), rnorm(20, 0, 0.2)) th    <- runif(20, 0, 2 * pi) outer <- cbind(2.0 * cos(th), 1.2 * sin(th)) + matrix(rnorm(40, 0, 0.1), 20) crd <- rbind(inner, outer) grp <- factor(c(rep("in", 20), rep("out", 20))) radialEllipse(crd, grp, fill = TRUE, add = FALSE) } # }

title: K-group radial-elliptic partition (nested ellipses) — radialEllipses
text: Generalises radialEllipse() k >= 2 groups using k-1 nested ellipses. nesting constraint (ellipse s-1 lies inside ellipse s) enforced optimisation. Groups ordered factor level (level 1 = innermost).

title: K-group radial-elliptic partition (nested ellipses) — radialEllipses
code: radialEllipses(   crd,   group,   ellipse = NULL,   fill = FALSE,   output = TRUE,   col = "purple",   cols = NULL,   lwd = 2,   lty = 1,   add = TRUE )

title: K-group radial-elliptic partition (nested ellipses) — radialEllipses
text: crd Numeric matrix data frame exactly 2 columns. group Factor k >= 2 levels. ellipse Optional length-5 numeric vector (cx, cy, , b, angle) specifying innermost ellipse exactly. supplied, outer ellipses uniform scalings one; scale factors searched. fill TRUE, shade elliptic sector. output TRUE (default), return results list. col Ellipse border colour (default "purple"). cols Length-k colours; auto-generated NULL. lwd Line width (default 2). lty Line type (default 1). add TRUE (default), add existing plot; FALSE, call plot() first.

title: K-group radial-elliptic partition (nested ellipses) — radialEllipses
text: output = TRUE, list vectors cx, cy, , b, angle (radians), misclass (list n indices), misclass_points (data frame columns x, y, label misclassified point), sector, majority.

title: K-group radial-elliptic partition (nested ellipses) — radialEllipses
text: ellipse supplied length-5 vector (cx, cy, , b, angle), defines innermost ellipse exactly. k-1 ellipses share center, orientation, :b ratio — outer ellipses uniform scalings inner one. scale factors t_2, ..., t_{k-1} (t_s > t_{s-1} >= 1) found exact 1D scan point's critical scale sqrt((u_i/)^2 + (v_i/b)^2) ellipse's rotated frame. ellipse NULL (default), ellipse independently optimised center, semi-axes, rotation, fitted Nelder-Mead. Multi-start uses covariance ellipse inner groups plus inflated copy previous ellipse (guaranteed- feasible starting point avoids stalling infeasibility penalty plateau).

title: K-group radial-elliptic partition (nested ellipses) — radialEllipses
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) g1 <- cbind(rnorm(15, 0, 0.2), rnorm(15, 0, 0.15)) th2 <- runif(15, 0, 2 * pi) g2 <- cbind(1.4 * cos(th2), 1.0 * sin(th2)) + matrix(rnorm(30, 0, 0.1), 15) th3 <- runif(15, 0, 2 * pi) g3 <- cbind(2.8 * cos(th3), 2.0 * sin(th3)) + matrix(rnorm(30, 0, 0.1), 15) crd <- rbind(g1, g2, g3) grp <- factor(c(rep("a", 15), rep("b", 15), rep("c", 15)))  # Independent ellipses radialEllipses(crd, grp, fill = TRUE, add = FALSE)  # Fixed-shape mode: supply the innermost ellipse as a 5-vector radialEllipses(crd, grp, ellipse = c(0, 0, 0.3, 0.2, 0), fill = TRUE, add = FALSE) } # }
